project.name: AnySchema
project.summary: Universal TypeScript schema utility package for validating, detecting, inferring, and converting schemas across common schema libraries.
goals: Provide a zero-runtime-dependency TypeScript package that normalizes validation, type inference, detection, and JSON Schema conversion across supported schema libraries.
Expose stable package exports for core helpers, adapter factories, built-in validators, built-in transformers, protocol types, and detection utilities.
Keep schema-library integrations adapter-based and structurally typed so consumers can use their own installed schema libraries without AnySchema owning those libraries.
nonGoals: Own product-specific validation rules, domain schemas, form behavior, HTTP API contracts, or persistence models for consuming projects.
Reimplement the schema libraries it adapts; AnySchema only detects, validates through, and transforms their public schema shapes.
Introduce runtime peer dependencies on consumer schema libraries or consumer-specific coupling.

owns.name: universal-schema-utility
owns.description: Core validation, parse, assert, type guard, vendor detection, metadata, and JSON Schema conversion helpers exported by the package.

owns.name: adapter-system
owns.description: Adapter interfaces, factory functions, helpers, and built-in validator/transformer adapters for supported schema vendors.

owns.name: anyschema-protocol
owns.description: Structural TypeScript protocol types and runtime detection for first-class AnySchema-compatible schemas.

doesNotOwn: Application or customer domain schemas that consume the package.
Runtime behavior, migration policy, or product workflows in repositories that use AnySchema.
Upstream schema library implementations, release lifecycles, or breaking-change policy.

allowedDependencies.surface: reusable release workflow and ADR-29 admission action
project manifest schema and engineering doctrine

forbiddenCouplings: Do not add application-specific validation rules or customer-specific schema behavior to the package core.
Do not import consumer repository internals, product schemas, runtime services, or persistence code.
Do not make supported schema vendors runtime dependencies of the published package unless a future ADR changes the zero-dependency contract.

delivery.deployPath: Pushes to main run .github/workflows/release.yml, which calls the SylphxAI/.github reusable release workflow and publishes the npm package.
delivery.productionProof: Release proof is a successful GitHub Release workflow on main plus npm package readback for @sylphx/anyschema at the released version.

gaps.description: Workflow emits ADR-29 fan-in contexts, but branch protection currently requires raw ci; migrate required contexts when central admission rollout reaches this repo.
Repo has README and ARCHITECTURE.md but no docs/adr or docs/specs tree yet; add ADRs/specs when the next material architecture or public-contract change occurs.
gaps.target: next material architecture or public-contract change
